basics.name: Charles Harrison Brammell III
basics.label: Computer Engineering Student
basics.summary: I am a Computer Engineering student at Auburn University with hands-on experience in MRI reconstruction research, digital hardware design, and software analytics.

work.name: Auburn Neuroimaging Research Center
work.position: Summer Undergraduate Research Fellow / Research Assistant
work.summary: I modified and optimized MRI reconstruction algorithms for muscle tissue while preparing technical findings for professional research audiences.
highlights: Modified and optimized MRI reconstruction algorithms to accommodate muscle tissue.
Analyzed Diffusion Tensor Imaging and Extended Phase Graph methods to improve post-processing.
Compared image correction methods and synthesized findings into a formal technical presentation.

work.name: See No Fees Financial
work.summary: I built internal and client-facing analytics tooling to support lead generation and sales staff training.
highlights: Developed custom tooling for data analytics internally and for clients.
Implemented data collection across internet properties for lead generation.
Prepared instructional material and trained sales staff on interpreting analytics.
Researched custom card processing solutions for clients with unique needs.

work.name: D’Livery NOLA
work.summary: I delivered goods with special handling requirements while maintaining on-time performance under dynamic scheduling demands.
highlights: Ensured timely delivery of temperature-controlled medications and other special-handling goods.
Completed emergency routes outside of scheduled hours with minimal notice.

projects.name: Interactive LLM Tokenizer Analysis Framework
projects.summary: I designed a modular evaluation framework to benchmark BPE, WordPiece, and SentencePiece tokenizers.
highlights: Built custom drivers to wrap tokenizers for scalable benchmarking.
Developed a toolbox for novel datasets, models, and evaluation metrics.
Evaluated tokenizer bias impact on processing speed, memory, and compute costs.

projects.name: FPGA AES Encryption Accelerator
projects.summary: I engineered a hardware-accelerated AES encryption module optimized for Nexys A7 FPGA deployment.
highlights: Designed system architecture in Vivado and implemented UART/SPI communication interfaces.
Built a robust serial interface bridging the FPGA with external data sources.
